title: asclepias crocea
intro: Asclepias crocea, also known as the yellow milkweed or yellow butterfly milkweed, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is known for its bright yellow flowers, which attract butterflies and other pollinators. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Asclepias crocea can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, salves, and teas, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, including colds, fever, and skin irritations.
